item.title: Sh’ma presents Meeting Worlds: Jazz, Folk, & Indian Classical
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm ⎮ Show: 7:30pm Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm ⎮ Show: 7:30pm

Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

Sh’ma

Sh’ma is the project of musicians Trevor Eulau and bassist Tony Lefaive, among other collaborators. At this concert, Sh’ma will perform their original music exploring meeting places between their Jazz backgrounds, with new interests in Indian Classical and Folk music. Tony and Trevor share a longtime musical connection.

Guitarist and composer Trevor Eulau has performed at such notable venues as the Bandcamp Headquarters, Timber Festival, KEXP Studios, among others. Trevor’s music is emotionally immediate, and imaginative, drawing from influences such as modern Jazz, meditative and spiritually rich world musics, as well as acoustic and folk sounds.

Trevor Eulau – Acoustic Guitar
Tony Lefaive – Upright and Electric Bass
Percussionist TBA

https://distrokid.com/hyperfollow/trevoreulau/my-purpose-seized-now

https://www.youtube.com/@trevoreulau

https://www.instagram.com/trevoreulau/


Opening group TBA

Join us for a special Happy Hour (the Happiest Hour), with lovely music, good food & drink, marvelous ambience, and some of your very best friends.

Happy Hour: 4:00 PM – 5:30 PM

Ticket: FREE – No cover, just great drinks and snacks

 

Seattle-based  trio Jim & Beth Wulff and Annie O’Neill  weave a dynamic musical landscape with lush rivers of sound and style .. whether they’re serenading you with jazz & sultry blues – or their  soulful original songs -this trio will get your toes tapping while taking you on ..’A Sentimental Journey..’ with some vintage jazz & more… creating the perfect ambience for the room.

item.title: The Joe Casalini Trio
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Join us for a special Happy Hour (the Happiest Hour), with lovely music, good food & drink, marvelous ambience, and […]
item.content:encoded:
Join us for a special Happy Hour (the Happiest Hour), with lovely music, good food & drink, marvelous ambience, and some of your very best friends.

Happy Hour: 4:00 PM – 5:30 PM
Ticket: FREE – No cover, just great drinks and snacks

The Casalini Trio has played happy hours at the Royal Room since 2019. They play laid-back, swinging versions of great tunes by Duke Ellington, Billy Strayhorn, Hoagie Carmichael, Phillip Johnston, and other great American songwriters of the last 100 years. The happy hour usually lasts about an hour and a half. Good drinks, good people, good ambience, and there’s never a cover charge, although the band often encourages donations to local food banks and other worthy causes.

item.title: We Rolling Block Party with Keep It Rolling Brass Band
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm ⎮ Show: 7:30pm Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm ⎮ Show: 7:30pm

Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

The Keep It Rolling Brass Band is a young, talented group of musicians from New Orleans, Louisiana. The band was founded in 2017 while the members were still in high school. They cut their chops the same way as most of the legendary brass bands of the past 50 years: by putting in time playing in storied locales like Jackson Square, Bourbon Street, the French Market, and Frenchmen Street. Playing for the city’s visitors in the Quarter, they soon developed a repertoire of songs that spans generations. In addition to the standard brass band songs that visitors to the city have come to expect, they play everything from Bobby Womack’s “Across 110th Street” to Mario’s “Let Me Love You.” Their professional success came quickly when they made their second-line debut at the Big 7 Social Aid and Pleasure Club’s 2018 parade and were subsequently cast as the featured brass band in Ciara’s music video for her 2019 single “Dose.” Since then, they’ve  performed for Solange and Rob49 and collaborated on brand partnerships with Nike and Under Armour. As the band has continued to work on their craft, they’ve added originals to their catalog that have become part of their signature sound. When you hear “Walker Like a Dog,” “Powerhouse,” or “Bourbon,” you can be sure you’re getting the well-honed sounds of the Keep It Rolling Brass Band.

We are just looking for a chance to show case our talent and have fun with everyone in Seattle by playing some great and energetic music.

Sue Quigley

item.title: Zoë’s Birthday Bash
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:00pm Tickets: $15 ADV, $20 DOS Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:00pm          Tickets: $15 ADV, $20 DOS

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

Come celebrate Zoë’s birthday with us at the Royal Room!!  Zoë has served and bartended at the Royal Room for four years and is hosting her 27th birthday party here with all of her favorite people. There will be live music with visuals, cake, photobooth, and a karaoke dance afterparty!!

Come at 7 pm to see some art rock by Sheridan Riley, Alvvays drummer and overall Seattle legend, in their own new band “Peg” with Carseat Headrest guitarist Ben Roth, also known as beloved Royal Room sound guy!

Next up we have Isaiah Banks, a mind-blowing up and up-and-coming Seattle singer. At only 21, he sounds like Miguel meets Erykah Badu!! Isaiah has been singing his whole life, growing up in gospel choirs, and has an unbelievable R&B sound.

To close the night, we have one of Zoë’s good friends Mario Fabrizio, prominent Seattle painter, bringing it home with his performance-art-heavy funk-rock project Star Gondola. Get ready to be mind blown, laugh, and maybe even cry, but definitely dance either way. Don’t miss an unforgettable night!!!

item.title: DESEO CARMIN Band and Friends
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm           Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

Prepare for an electrifying evening of rhythm, passion, and global sound as DESEO CARMIN, Seattle‑based award‑winning Latin Jazz–Funk–Flamenco fusion band, brings its high‑octane performance to the stage. Known for their fiery musicianship, theatrical flair, and genre‑bending sound—often described as “Spanish music with an edge”—the band promises an unforgettable night of music and dance.

Led by Stella Rossi, Paraguayan‑born vocalist, dancer, composer, and multi‑instrumentalist, DESEO CARMIN delivers a powerhouse performance blending virtuosic guitar work, driving bass lines, dynamic percussion, and passionate flamenco dance. Rossi is joined by core ensemble members Andrei Stomma on flamenco and electric guitar and Camilo Estrada on bass guitar.

The evening will also feature an outstanding lineup of special guest artists, including Rafael Tranquilino, award‑winning blues guitarist; Bill Ray, legendary drummer; Alessandro Scott on bass guitar; and additional surprise guest performers—adding fresh sparks of spontaneity to an already explosive experience.

DESEO CARMIN has captivated audiences at major venues including Benaroya Hall, McCaw Hall, The Broadway Center for the Performing Arts, and The Triple Door, and has been featured in Seattle’s Out to Lunch concert series and the Seattle Latino Film Festival Opening Gala. Their work has appeared on NBC’s KING 5, KCTS9, Seattle Channel’s Art Zone, as well as international television in Paraguay and Chile.

The Blues have long been a rally cry transforming personal and collective hardships – stemming from racial injustice and systemic oppression – into a musical language of resistance and essential expression. Rooted in field hollers and call-and-response, these songs provided emotional resilience and solidarity within Black communities. Continue the journey with us as we explore historic and current ideations of this truly American art form.

Featuring Adra Boo, Aline Vida, Taryn Dorsey & Tiffany Wilson

item.title: Alice Howe & Freebo with Special Guest Jeff Fielder
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: Doors 6:30pm | Show 7:30pm Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors 6:30pm | Show 7:30pm     Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

Alice Howe & Freebo have undeniable chemistry. Rock bass legend Freebo weaves his fretless stylings into Alice’s soulful, impeccably-tuned vocals for a harmony-driven performance showcasing two uniquely compelling songwriters.

Freebo is a genuine folk, rock, and blues icon who has toured and recorded with some of the greatest artists of his generation, and is best known for his ten years as bassist with Bonnie Raitt. His fretless bass can be heard on records by John Mayall, Ringo Starr, Crosby Stills & Nash, Maria Muldaur, Joe Walsh, Dr. John, and many more. Freebo is also a multi award-winner and finalist in numerous songwriting contests, was recognized as Best Folk Artist by the Los Angeles Music Awards, and has appeared on Saturday Night Live, The Muppet Show, The Midnight Special, and in concert with the Legendary Spinal Tap.

Alice Howe had the honor of being named Best Female Artist at the International Acoustic Music Awards. To hear her sing is to be enraptured by the natural, unaffected beauty of her voice. There’s no artifice, no histrionics — just honest, authentic, emotionally resonant singing in the tradition of the roots music that shaped her. Her poetic lyrics and melodic sensibilities take center stage on her latest solo album Circumstance, produced by Freebo and recorded at legendary FAME Studios in Muscle Shoals, AL. Americana Highways writes of Circumstance: “One of 2023’s best albums. This isn’t just a singer – this is an artist.”

Alice & Freebo have been working as a duo since 2017, and together they tour relentlessly, playing over 85 shows annually in both 2024 and 2025 across US and Europe. “A beguiling blend of folk, rock, blues, and soul,” their collaboration reveals a deep musical kinship that transcends their generational divide. Their latest album Alice Howe & Freebo Live debuted as the #1 Most Played Album on the Folk Radio Charts, and also landed on its Top Ten Albums of 2025.

“Together, on stage, Howe and Freebo are the perfect fit.” – Americana UK

“When these two get together, prepare yourself for an extraordinary evening of song and smiles, and maybe a tear or two. Powerful songwriting, mesmerizing vocals, outstanding musicianship, and a palpable camaraderie make for an experience unsurpassed on the contemporary acoustic scene.” – The Folk Music Notebook

item.title: “The Work” A May Day Benefit Concert for OneAmerica
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: Sponsored by South Hudson Music Project Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm Donations to One America are encouraged! […]
item.content:encoded:
Sponsored by South Hudson Music Project

Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm          Donations to One America are encouraged!

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

The origins of May Day can be traced back to international labor organizing in the late 19th century. The observance originated from a movement demanding improved working conditions and greater recognition for workers’ contributions. In 1884, a national federation of unions announced a campaign to establish an eight-hour workday by May 1, 1886. Workers in cities across North America went on strike leading up to that date in one of the era’s largest and most tumultuous periods of worker unrest.

FEATURING:
Jourdan Keith – poet
Ben Hunter & Joe Seamons
Havilah Rand
Reggie Garrett & Christine Gunn
Akemi
Amber Wolfe & Mark Lutwak
Fandangueros de Seattle

ABOUT ONEAMERICA:

We rise together.

OneAmerica builds leadership and organizes grassroots leaders and our allies in key areas in Washington state to push for policy change, mobilize civic engagement in our communities and advocate for systems that welcome and include immigrants at every level.

Immigrant and refugee community members like us lead our movement for immigrant power and collective change, because we are best equipped to create solutions that build lasting power for our communities.

VISION – OneAmerica envisions a peaceful world where every person’s human rights and dignity are respected, where communities appreciate differences and stand together for justice and equality, and where each person contributes to the common good.

MISSION – OneAmerica advances the fundamental principles of democracy and justice at the local, state, and national levels by building power within immigrant communities in collaboration with key allies.

The Horsenecks

Based in Astoria, Oregon, The Horsenecks are Gabrielle Macrae and Barry Southern. They are two multi-instrumentalists and harmony singers who have come together from traditional music backgrounds and found a sound that weaves through layers of influence that include early Appalachian stringband music, classic country, bluegrass and original folk.

Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

Som da Massa Album Release

A transcontinental collaboration between Ju, a Seattle-based pernambucana, and Sky, a Massachusetts-based gringo, Som da Massa is the sound of these things. The project brings Northeastern and Northern Brazilian Northeastern traditions like baião, ciranda, ijexá, and carimbó into conversation with 70s-inflected MPB and contemporary international styles, weaving stories, rhythms, and voices across borders.

This event will be the launch of their latest album: “O Amor, O Sonho e A Dor“

Bebop, Boleros & Bossa:
Robin Kallsen & Laura Steeves

Laura Steeves and Robin Kallsen are two vocalists who met in the Seattle jazz jam scene and discovered their shared love for the music of Latin America: the habanera, the bossa, the bolero, the Peruvian waltz, and so much more. They’re teaming up to share their favorite Latin tunes with a jazzy twist that hearkens back to Laura’s childhood soundscape. Featuring Rob Lowe on guitar, Kyle Doran on drums, and Osama Afifi on bass.

item.title: The Quilt Sessions: April
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Program: 1–5 PM: Day-time small-group sessions 7–9 PM: Evening large-group sessions RSVP: Donations to Northwest Folklife welcome! Tickets do not […]
item.content:encoded:
Program:

1–5 PM: Day-time small-group sessions
7–9 PM: Evening large-group sessions

RSVP: Donations to Northwest Folklife welcome!

Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After you RSVP on Stranger Tickets please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance RSVP and are prioritized for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

Northwest Folklife is is a 501(c)(3) heritage organization that celebrates the diverse tapestry of arts, cultures, and traditions emanating from a global Pacific Northwest. Since 1971, NW Folklife’s work has been rooted in the shared humanity of connection and creative expression and is driven by intergenerational, intercultural experiences. Our staff collaborates with more than 100 Community Coordinators—including respected artists, educators, tradition bearers, and cultural leaders throughout the region— to envision, sow, and nurture collective opportunities, including the 55-year-old Northwest Folklife Festival, our Annual Cultural Focus (2026 -“Ubuntu”), the Cultural & Creative Workforce Development Program and Our Big Neighborhood events. The organization and its diverse communities continue to grow and evolve, reclaiming “folk” as the vibrant, wise, and present future that resides in all of us.

The Quilt Sessions are a dynamic collection of artistry, knowledge sharing and cultural preservation that are accessible to community through streaming events and the hosted on Northwest Folklife’s Archive.

April series: 4/7, 4/14, 4/21, and 4/28

DONATE TODAY!

Happy Hour: 4:00 PM – 5:30 PM
Ticket: FREE – No cover, just great drinks and snacks

The Casalini Trio has played happy hours at the Royal Room since 2019. They play laid-back, swinging versions of great tunes by Duke Ellington, Billy Strayhorn, Hoagie Carmichael, Phillip Johnston, and other great American songwriters of the last 100 years. The happy hour usually lasts about an hour and a half. Good drinks, good people, good ambience, and there’s never a cover charge, although the band often encourages donations to local food banks and other worthy causes.

We’re All Neighbors
Music Benefit for Riverton Church Migrant Arrival Support

with: Braly-Sangster, Liberty Strikes, and kkollective!

Riverton Church in Tukwila provides first line support for immigrants seeking asylum. This includes legal advice, housing, food, employment and social services.

kkollective! A joy-based project by Karin Kallander (Last Minute Maybes) to build community through music and to raise awareness and funds for Just Causes! Karin brings in different local musicians to play an eclectic and groovy set of originals and inspiring covers.

Braly-Sangster with Keith Lowe and Kevin Warner
Leslie Braly and Johnny Sangster have been singing and writing songs for decades in the Seattle music scene. Their harmonies together transcend and alchemize into the multiplicity of selves present in each of us.

Liberty Strikes
Faith Stankevich and her 9-piece performance band speak out for justice and give voice to the voiceless while YOU get up and dance!

item.title: Clive Carroll
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm | Show: 7:30pm Tickets: $20 advance | $25 doors Advance tickets […]

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone.

Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

The Royal Room is All Ages until 10pm. 

Clive Carroll

Clive Carroll has established himself as one of the world’s premier acoustic guitar players.  He has toured across Europe, Australia, the Middle East and North America, garnering praise for his sublime performances of everything from 16th and 17th century lute music to Jazz standards, Blues, Irish reels and his own groundbreaking compositions. Lauded guitarist Tommy Emmanuel has also taken Clive on the road, and similar nods to Clive’s musicianship have been given by everyone from classical guitarist John Williams to Madonna, Guy Ritchie, Michelin award-winning chef Jean-Christophe Novelli, and the Sultanate of Oman.

https://linktr.ee/clivecarroll

Clive Carroll’s unique tone, versatility, and unmistakeable voice as a composer, have seen him become one of the world’s premier acoustic guitar players and “…arguably the most accomplished fingerstylist in the world today…” (Total Guitar Magazine).

item.title: Urban Naturalist Lecture Series: Adrian Wolf
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Doors: 5:00pm Lecture: 6:00pm FREE This event is completely free to attend! If you find value in tonight’s lecture, […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 5:00pm     Lecture: 6:00pm

FREE

This event is completely free to attend! If you find value in tonight’s lecture, we warmly encourage you to make a donation at the end. Your generosity helps us continue bringing these experiences to the community.

Seating is first-come, first-served. Register here!

Wild In Seattle | David B. Williams

Join us for a fascinating evening with Adrian Wolf, Stewardship Director at Great Peninsula Conservancy and a lead member of the innovative Listen Up Collaborative, a regional partnership using cutting-edge acoustic monitoring and A.I. to understand the health of Western Washington’s forests.

Adrian’s work—recently spotlighted in national environmental coverage—centers on a simple but powerful idea: birds are some of our most insightful ecological storytellers. By installing small audio monitors across more than 4,500 acres of protected lands, Adrian and his collaborators have collected hundreds of thousands of bird recordings that reveal how forest restoration efforts are working in real time. These recordings are analyzed with artificial intelligence to identify species such as the Pacific wren, spotted towhee, and chestnut‑backed chickadee, providing a data-rich understanding of habitat recovery.

This collaborative effort—uniting land trusts, tribal partners, and county parks—has grown into a model for climate‑resilient conservation. Through practices like selective thinning, brush management, and the creation of habitat features like snags and brush piles, the team is restoring ecological structure across diverse landscapes, from floodplains to former timberlands. Their acoustic monitoring allows them to measure whether wildlife is truly benefiting from these interventions, replacing anecdotal observations with precise long‑term data.

In this talk, Adrian will take us inside the science and the stories behind this work—what birds are telling us about our forests, why their “nature’s Yelp reviews” matter for conservation planning, and how new technologies are helping land stewards respond to climate change with greater accuracy and care.

Whether you’re a birder, a climate‑curious learner, or someone who simply loves the natural world, this program offers a compelling look at how listening deeply to birds can shape the future of forest restoration in the Pacific Northwest.

This event is brought to you from the Seward Park Audubon Center

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone.

Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

The Royal Room is All Ages until 10pm. 

This event is a benefit for Donkeysaddle Projects and their work with families in Gaza.

Klezmer Starts Here!
A Benefit for Amara with Seattle Fiddlesticks and the Music of Herman S. Shapiro

Klezmer Starts Here is a curated series at Seattle’s Royal Room presented by the Klein Party and South Hudson Music Project featuring local and national klezmer musicians exploring the breadth and depth of Yiddish music.

A benefit for Amara, an organization that partners with people and communities most impacted by our child welfare system, to address trauma, build connections, and ultimately ensure all children, adults and families feel safe and supported, with a commitment to equity every step of the way. The concert will feature 2 sets of klezmer, an instrumental musical tradition of the Jews of Central and Eastern Europe going back hundreds of years. One set will be performed by Seattle Fiddlesticks, an ensemble of young musicians between the ages of 8 and 18. The second set will be a performance of music written and published by Herman S. Shapiro in 1902, that has not been heard for over 100 years. It will be performed by a “supergroup” of Seattle klezmer musicians drawn from several ensembles, including Kesselgarden, the Klein Party, the Talne Trio, and Tzepl.

item.title: Abby Posner and Paula Fong
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm | Show: 7:30pm Tickets: $20 advance | $25 doors Advance tickets […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm  |  Show: 7:30pm                Tickets: $20 advance  |  $25 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone.

Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

The Royal Room is All Ages until 10pm. 

Abby Posner and Paula Fong

Abby Posner is a multi-instrumentalist from Los Angeles whose energy is comparable to Chris Thile and The Lumineers. Her songs range from intimate, haunting folk songs, to upbeat festival tunes. Her music video Quiet on Sunset was featured on CMT, she scored the music for 4 documentaries including Lady Buds. She received Official Showcases at Americana Fest 2023, and Folk Alliance International 2022 and 2024 and 2025. Winner of USA Songwriting Competition for her song Get Loud co-written with Ships Have Sailed, and winner of Topanga Fiddle Festival’s Best Song award for her song ‘Blind spots’ in 2020. Abby will be joined by talented bandmate and singer songwriter Paula Fong for this show.

Los Angeles singer-songwriter Paula Fong commands attention the moment she takes the stage. She pairs pure-toned clarity with a soulful power that defies her small stature. Her sound traverses the full landscape of roots music – from stark Gillian Welch-style acoustic to country swing to rockin singer/songwriter strength. Her genre versatility draws comparisons to Patty Griffin, Linda Ronstadt and Karla Bonoff. Whether performing solo with acoustic guitar, alongside multi-instrumentalist Phil Glenn (Moonsville, Show Ponies), or with a full band, Fong captivates with warmth of her surprising intensity and grit. The single “Ophelia” from her debut EP Chestnut Mare earned International Acoustic Music Award recognition, with 2026 bringing a bold two-part 10 song album produced by Justin James Sinclair (ROSIE, Jenny Owen Youngs, Weezer, Kate Micucci, Train).

item.title: Peter Mulvey & Katie Dahl
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm | Show: 7:30pm Tickets: $20 advance | $25 doors Advance tickets […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm  |  Show: 7:30pm                Tickets: $20 advance  |  $25 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone.

Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

The Royal Room is All Ages until 10pm. 

Peter Mulvey & Katie Dahl

Peter Mulvey and Katie Dahl bring their world-class songwriting, sharp humor, and deep empathy to the Royal Room for one night only.

Peter Mulvey has been a songwriter, road-dog, raconteur and almost-poet since before he can remember. Raised working-class Catholic on the Northwest side of Milwaukee, he took a semester in Ireland, and immediately began cutting classes to busk on Grafton Street in Dublin and hitchhike through the country, finding whatever gigs he could. Back stateside, he spent a couple years gigging in the Midwest before lighting out for Boston, where he returned to busking (this time in the subway) and coffeehouses. Small shows led to larger shows, which eventually led to regional and then national and international touring. The wheels have not stopped since.

Clear-eyed and tough-minded, songwriter/playwright Katie Dahl is known for her smart songs, wry wit, and wise spirit. A small-town celebrity on the Wisconsin peninsula where her family has lived for 175 years, Katie is also an internationally touring, radio-charting artist who “delivers razor-sharp lyrics with a hearty, soulful voice” (American Songwriter). In live shows that are both courageously honest and devilishly funny, Katie dives deep into questions of land and love, family and body image, grief and joy.

“Katie Dahl’s songs aren’t just melodies and words,” says Dar Williams. “They’re journeys that are firmly grounded in a sense of place–beautiful, real landscapes that help you feel places that you may have never been before. That’s the very best kind of songwriting.”

item.title: Happy Hour with Sheryl Wiser
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Happy Hour from 4pm to 5:30pm The happy hour usually lasts about an hour and a half. Good drinks, good […]
item.content:encoded:
Happy Hour from 4pm to 5:30pm

The happy hour usually lasts about an hour and a half. Good drinks, good people, good ambience, and there’s never a cover charge, although the band often encourages donations to local food banks and other worthy causes.

Sheryl Wiser

Seattle-based singer, songwriter, and guitarist Sheryl Wiser has crafted a distinctive and compelling blend of Americana, folk-jazz, roots, and blues. Armed with her signature Parker Fly guitar, her rhythmic guitar style melds beautifully with warm, vibrant vocals and evocative lyrics that fearlessly explore universal themes of love, loss, resilience, connection, and belonging. Her music is both deeply personal and relatable, with an ability to connect with audiences in a multitude of spaces – from intimate house concerts to venue and festival stages.

A veteran of the 1990s alt-country/Americana band Somebody’s Daughter, over the years, she has become a well-regarded solo artist, continuing to hone her songwriting by combining keen observations with emotional richness and depth. The Seattle Times writes, “Wiser’s music is sharp and clean, featuring her sweet, powerful voice tempered by rich, stunning melody. She writes emotional, rhythmically stirring songs and delivers them with genuine passion, and is a gifted singer and songwriter.”

A long-time advocate for local food and farms in WA state, she continues to shift towards a full-time music career, with 2025 seeing notable growth, playing over 40 shows, including the Tractor Tavern, Royal Room, and Rabbit Box Theatre, along with regional festivals, and other unique spaces, including Seattle-Tacoma International Airport’s “Music at Sea” program. She also continues to perform through her philanthropic house concert series Pie + Persistence, which has raised over $120,000 for more than 34 non-profits since 2017.

Nominated for a 2026 Sonic Guild Seattle Artists Grant, she begins work on her first album in 30 years with noted songwriter, composer, and multi-instrumentalist Tomo Nakayama in January 2026, with a release date slated for later in the year.

item.title: Kavita Shah Quintet
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm. Set 1: 7:30pm; Set 2: 8:30pm Tickets: $30 advance, $35 doors Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm. Set 1: 7:30pm; Set 2: 8:30pm

Tickets: $30 advance, $35 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

Kavita Shah Quintet

Kavita Shah is an award-winning vocalist, composer, researcher and polyglot who makes globally-minded music that is deeply engaged with the jazz tradition. Her winding path back to her ancestral homeland is embodied in the title of the Kavita Shah Quintet’s forthcoming album, All Roads Lead to Home, coming in Winter 2026. A lifelong New Yorker of Indian origin hailed for possessing an “amazing dexterity for musical languages” (NPR), Shah incorporates her ethnographic research on Brazilian, West African, and Indian traditions into her original music, and she regularly tours her music on six continents. Her latest album, All Roads Lead to Home, features a global cast of established New York musicians. Joining her in Seattle are pianist Gabriel Chakarji, guitarist Vinícius Gomes, bassist Devon Gates, and drummer Ele Howell.

“Shah’s music reflects the insatiably curious mind of an ethnographer,
the soul of a poet, and the eye of a painter.”
– Owen McNally, WNPR

The Quintet’s music is brimming with complex rhythms and soulful songs that carry stories of the natural world and the hidden songlines connecting seemingly disparate cultures — from a Gujarati lullaby about sparrows to an Argentine novelist in exile in Paris; from a Brazilian sea goddess to the peacocks roaming freely on Shah’s ancestral farmland. All Roads Lead to Home marks a clear arrival of Shah’s lyrical and compositional voice — one that seamlessly blends global folk music into the language of modern jazz.

Kavita Shah – Voice
Vinícius Gomes – Guitar
Gabriel Chakarji – Piano
Devon Gates – Bass
Ele Howell – Drums

Jazz Underground is a Seattle-based contemporary jazz big band.  Their repertoire includes a wide variety of music from Count Basie, Buddy Rich and Stan Kenton to Jaco Pastorius, Michael League, Maria Schneider, Greg Adams, Frank Zappa, Tom Molter and Nelda Swiggett.  In addition, the group performs music by Pacific Northwest composers and arrangers, including original tunes by members of the band.

item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele

item.content:encoded:

Tickets: $15 advance | $20 door
free entrance to kids 12 and under

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

Brazilian Showcase & Solstice Parade Launch
ft. Adriana Giordano, Bloco Pacífico & DJ Tomás

Experience an electrifying evening of Brazilian music, rhythm, and dance at the Brazilian Showcase & Solstice Parade Launch.

Led by vocalist Adriana Giordano, the evening features a dynamic live set with her band, bringing the richness and soul of Brazilian music to life. The celebration continues with powerful percussion and vibrant energy from Bloco Pacífico musicians, capturing the spirit of Brazil’s street traditions.

Adding to the excitement, dancer Deise Costa will take the stage with captivating performances that embody the movement, color, and joy of Brazilian culture.

Keeping the energy high throughout the night, DJ Tomás will be spinning an irresistible mix of Brazilian beats and global grooves—bridging live performances with a dancefloor vibe that keeps everyone moving.

This special event also marks the launch of the Solstice Parade, inviting the community to come together, celebrate, and participate in this beloved Seattle tradition.

We will also be celebrating the birthday of our dear friend Cassia Weber, a truly charismatic presence in our community who is deeply cherished by all of us, also lovingly known as Cassinha.

After the performances, the stage opens up for an open jam—welcoming musicians, dancers, poets, and creatives of all kinds to join in, collaborate, and keep the energy flowing.

Join us for a night of music, dance, and community—full of rhythm, connection, and festive energy.

Adriana Giordano – vocals
Gabriel Rodrigues – keys
Dean Schmidt – bass
Gui Fonseca – guitar
Jeff Busch – drums

SAM COBRA

Sam Cobra is a four piece female-fronted band from Seattle. Their music blends heavy psych rock with ethereal vocals to create a sound uniquely their own. All of their songs are part of a single storyline that is threaded throughout their lyrics and three studio albums. The band plays regularly throughout Seattle and has toured Washington and Oregon. They are currently working on new music to release in 2026.

item.description: Doors: 6:00pm Show: 7:00pm  […]

Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

The Royal Room is All Ages until 10pm. 

 Wanderers By Trade

Celebrate the enduring legacy of Nobel Prize-winning icon, Bob Dylan, on his 85th birthday with an immersive journey through a career that redefined the landscape of American music.  Wanderers By Trade dives deep into the lyrical and poetic genius of Bob Dylan. This specially tailored performance showcases the evolution of his sound and message. Spoken word artist Seanjohn Walsh completes the show with an epic performance of Dylan’s lyrics.

Dylan’s music is more relevant than ever. In an age of complexity, his lyrics offer a mirror to the world and a window into the human spirit. There is no better place to experience this purposeful retrospective than the intimate and acoustically superb setting of The Royal Room.

item.title: The Naughty Novel Social Club presents: Heated Rivals
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm           Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

The Naughty Novel Social Club

A night of live sportsball themed romantasy readings, burlesque, Queer Cheer Squad, drinks & food and all around smutty fun! The Naughty Novel Social Club is ready to score, inspired by all the sweat, dirty texting and cottage dreams of Heated Rivalry. This full contact sport night will titillate, tease and touch your heart. Silly pleasure activism at its best!

item.title: The Nate Omdal Septet plays the Music from “Star Wars”
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: Doors: 9:00pm Show: Soon After Tickets: $15 advance, $20 doors Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 9:00pm  Show: Soon After           Tickets: $15 advance, $20 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

 

The Nate Omdal Septet plays music from “Star Wars”

Bassist, arranger and bandleader Nate Omdal has put together a unique set of music from everyone’s favorite tale from a galaxy far, far away… just in time for International Star Wars Day!! Featuring Walter Cano (trp) Kate Olson (sax) Brian Bermudez (sax) Audrey Stangland (trombone) Michael Owcharuk (keys) and Cody Rahn (drums), this evening will be more fun that shooting womp-rats with a T-16. Wear something fun or bring something that lights up and makes blaster sounds!

item.title: Panda Conspiracy with special guest Jammah
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm           Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

Panda Conspiracy – New Music Listening Session
with special guest Jammah

Panda Conspiracy plays The Royal Room for a special seated evening debuting new material the band has been developing in recent months. Known for their high-energy, horn-driven sound, this performance brings that same dynamic range into a more intimate listening room setting—highlighting new arrangements, deeper grooves, and space to stretch out musically.

Panda Conspiracy will present a featured early set, followed by an instrumental funk set from Jammah, led by Jason Cressy.

Advance tickets are recommended to ensure a table. Come early, enjoy dinner and drinks, and settle in for an evening of fresh music and deep grooves.

item.title: Cascadia Big Band Sinatra Show
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we […]

Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

Cascadia Big Band is proud to bring back our famous Sinatra show featuring our very own Frank Sinatra in Roger Bare. This show celebrates the infamous 1966 “Sinatra at the Sands” recording with the Count Basie band conducted and arranged by Quincy Jones. We will be featuring many of those charts and others all done by “Ol Blue Eyes”. This show has sold out in the past so don’t wait to get your tickets.

item.title: Heart Matter
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm | Show: 7:30pm Tickets: $20 in advance | $25 at the door Advance tickets can only be purchased […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm | Show: 7:30pm

Tickets: $20 in advance |  $25 at the door

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone.

Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

The Royal Room is All Ages until 10pm.

HEART MATTER

Catch SF Bay Area based band, Heart Matter, featuring vocalist Amy D and vibraphonist Dillon Vado in their first appearance at The Royal Room in April! As part of a West Coast Tour, this show features their quartet and original music from their forthcoming debut album, Horizons.

Heart Matter has created a truly unique sonic footprint with a jazz-informed, indie band feel. Now with live processing of the vibraphone through guitar pedals, their music is sculpted in real time; setting the stage for riveting and emotionally-driven compositions.

With songs that center honesty and reverence for the human experience, Heart Matter reckons with loss and encourages presence and deeper connection. Co-led by Amy D and Dillon Vado, the two composers bring a genuine collaborative spirit to the music. Accomplished Bay Area musicians Lukas Vesely (bass) and Isaac Schwartz (drums) complete the lineup and serve the music with precision and spontaneity.

Having built a following through performances at SFJAZZ, Healdsburg Jazz Festival, and the San Jose Jazz Summer Fest, they’re a band that’s worth catching live. Think Joni Mitchell’s lyrical directness, Esperanza Spalding’s adventurous yet accessible musicality, and the dynamic, avant garde qualities of The Bad Plus. Audiences often experience laughter, tears, and quiet reflection at their shows, describing the music as:

“fiery and exciting,”

“deep, elegant, honest,”

“the meeting point of impeccable musicianship and catharsis.”

More than anything, Heart Matter seeks to share transformative music with those who need to hear it, offering a remedy for the trance of modern life and a sacred space to feel it all.

item.title: STEBMO / THC.XLR
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm | Show: 7:30pm  […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm | Show: 7:30pm                                  Tickets: $20 in advance |  $25 at the door

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone.

Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

The Royal Room is All Ages until 10pm.

STEBMO is Steve Moore, a multi-instrumentalist hailing from Seattle, WA. Known as a pianist, with a love for wurlitzers, casiotones and bells, he is also a trombonist and composer. As a studio musician and sideman, he has a resume that reads like a cult top-10 list with artists as diverse as songwriter Sufjan Stevens, jazz hero Bill Frisell and black metal mavericks sunnO))).

THC.XLR is a synthesis of the natural and electronic world. With Madi Levine (IVVY) on drum set and percussion, Erik Sanchez (Richie Dagger’s Crime) on synth/sampler, and Rob Hanlon (Sketch Artist) on saxophone, piano, electronics. Take a deep hit, and expect flavors of jazz, drone, ambient/experimental and Dub.

Madi Levine – drum set and percussion
Erik Sanchez – synthesizer and sampler

https://thc-xlr.bandcamp.com/album/the-knowledge-ep

Presented by Groundhum

https://www.instagram.com/groundhum/

item.title: An Evening with Stillhouse Junkies
item.dc:creator: Sherry Steele
item.description: Doors: 6:00pm Show: 7:00pm  […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:00pm Show: 7:00pm                                                         Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone.

Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

The Royal Room is All Ages until 10pm. 

STILLHOUSE JUNKIES

“A sound that rings with resilience and resolve.” — Bluegrass Today

Acoustic adventurers Stillhouse Junkies explore the worlds between roots, bluegrass, Texas swing, blues, and rock. Their free-flowing musical interplay and improvisation make every show unique as the trio weave through high-energy, intricately composed original songs, never taking the same path twice. Formed in 2017, the band consists of Fred Kosak (guitar, mandolin), Alissa Wolf (fiddle), and Jeanette Adams (bass).

item.title: Deidre McCalla & Joe Jencks
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm           Tickets: $20 advance, $25 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone. Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

Deidre McCalla & Joe Jencks. Two award-winning songwriters. Two master storytellers. Together they weave a tapestry of song bridging cultures, communities, and traditions.

Deidre McCalla – Black woman, lesbian, feminist, mother – crafts songs with nuanced reflections on love, family, justice, and belonging that resonate deeply with listeners across identities.

Joe Jencks, a dual U.S./Irish citizen and a 25 year veteran of the international Folk circuit, is a multi-instrumentalist delivering engaging musical narratives and songs of social consciousness filled with heart, soul, and grit.

This will be the evening your heart didn’t know it needed.

www.deidremccalla.com
www.joejencks.com

YouTube: DeidreMcCallaMusic
YouTube: @joejencksmusic-official

item.title: Nicholas Krolak – PAST LIVES Album Release Concert
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm Tickets: $25 advance, $30 doors Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at […]
item.content:encoded:
Doors: 6:30pm Show: 7:30pm

Tickets: $25 advance, $30 doors

Advance tickets can only be purchased online-we do not sell advance tickets at the venue. Refunds are not available within 48 hours of the event. Tickets do not guarantee seating during shows at the Royal Room. 

We are now accepting reservations for diners! After purchasing tickets, please visit the Reservations page to book a table. Table reservations require advance tickets, and are only for guests who plan to dine at the Royal Room.  We do not take reservations over the phone.

Seating for non-diners is first come, first served. Please arrive early to guarantee a seat!

The Royal Room is All Ages until 10pm. 

Nicholas Krolak

“Somewhere between Charles Mingus and Johnny Greenwood there is a sound that bassist/composer Nicholas Krolak seeks. A young veteran of the Philadelphia music scene. He has spent the last decade working as a side-man, experiencing new styles, and studying with the masters. Now, he has taken all he has learned and is applying it to his own artistic adventures. Tracing the lineage of bebop into the contemporary, while constantly balancing the pulse of the city and the mysticism of nature: he has been described as… a different kind of songwriter—perhaps a different kind of jazz musician altogether. He’s like the bass playing, modern embodiment of Walt Whitman. And, just as Whitman used his prose poems, Krolak uses his music to celebrate and also reconcile the competing and contradictory nature of the two worlds he straddles: the natural and the urban.”
Matt Silver – WRTI

This tour exists inside the world of PAST LIVES.
It takes shape in rooms where listening changes … where sound lingers, attention sharpens, and the space between notes matters. These are not ordinary nights. They unfold gradually, shaped by the room, the people in it, and whatever arrives in the moment.

Nothing is rushed …
Nothing is repeated …
The music is allowed to breathe, to stretch, to move time in unfamiliar ways. What happens is shared, fleeting, and difficult to describe afterward … something you feel more than remember.
These gatherings are uncommon, with limited space by design.
If this resonates, you’re invited …
There’s just one catch … tickets are limited … don’t wait … see you there !

instagram @nicholas_krolak
